---

### Go SDK Generation Failure

**Pipeline:** https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/internal/_build/results?buildId=6194453

**Root cause:** The Go SDK generation failed during `go mod tidy` because the emitter attempted to generate `v3` of the `armservicefabric` module, but the module `github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/resourcemanager/servicefabric/armservicefabric/v3` does not yet exist in the Go module registry:

```
go: github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/resourcemanager/servicefabric/armservicefabric/v3:
module github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go@latest found (v68.0.0+incompatible),
but does not contain package github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/resourcemanager/servicefabric/armservicefabric/v3
```

The `tsp-client` initialization and code generation succeeded, but `go mod tidy` failed because the generated code references the new v3 module path that hasn't been published yet.

---

### Troubleshooting the Go SDK Failure

> To troubleshoot SDK generation failures locally:
>
> 1. Clone the [azure-rest-api-specs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs) repository.
> 2. Open the cloned repository in VS Code and use GitHub Copilot Chat with a prompt like "generate SDK locally for Go" to troubleshoot the error.
> 3. Make any required TypeSpec changes to fix the SDK generation errors, then re-trigger SDK generation.

---
